action_items.issue: H1b p=0.028 cited as evidence despite failing MCP
action_items.description: Remove all uncorrected p-values from results. H1b_L8_Pearson raw p=0.028 becomes p=0.334 after Bonferroni (12 tests, alpha=0.00417). BH-FDR q=0.107 also fails q<0.05.
specific_changes: Line 61: Remove 'r=-0.431, p=0.028 for delta-corrected steering, H1b' as evidence
Line 284: Remove 'H1b: r=-0.431, p=0.028 (uncorrected)' from Table 5 or mark as non-significant after MCP
Add explicit statement: 'Zero hypotheses survived multiple comparison correction (Bonferroni alpha=0.00417 for 12 tests)'

action_items.description: The title claims 'Feature Absorption as Optimal Compression' but H6 (primary predictive hypothesis) is falsified (precision@20=0.0). The LCA-SAE correspondence is mechanistic, not empirical.
specific_changes: Option A: Rename to 'Feature Absorption Does Not Degrade SAE Interpretability: Null Results and Metric Validation'
Option B: Rename to 'Competitive Suppression as the Mechanism of SAE Feature Absorption' with explicit H6 falsification in subtitle
Do not claim optimal compression framing as empirical contribution when its main prediction failed

action_items.description: Abstract leads with LCA-SAE correspondence and graph predictions that failed. Should lead with null results and metric validation.
specific_changes: Lead paragraph: State 'Zero hypotheses survived multiple comparison correction'
Add metric validation: 'Random SAEs show 8x higher measured absorption (0.278 vs 0.034), suggesting metric sensitivity to structure'
Third: Introduce mechanistic framework as explanation for null results, not primary contribution
Remove 'precision@20=0.0' framing as 'primary hypothesis falsified' - make it explicit the graph does not work

action_items.description: Section 4.5 performs post-hoc power analysis ('approximately 20% power'). This is methodologically invalid.
specific_changes: Remove all post-hoc power analysis
Replace with pre-hoc power statement if needed: 'A priori power (alpha=0.05, n=26, medium effect r=0.5) approximately 47%'

action_items.description: Chanin metric shows 8x higher absorption in random SAEs. Paper assumes this means trained SAEs have reduced absorption pathology, but could mean metric is structure-sensitive.
specific_changes: Add explicit discussion of three interpretations of H10 result
Conclude: 'We cannot distinguish between metric sensitivity to structure vs. detection of learned pathology reduction with current data'

action_items.description: H6 (primary predictive hypothesis) failed completely (precision@20=0.0, p=1.0) but is treated as nuance rather than central finding.
specific_changes: Elevate H6 falsification to first-class result in abstract and conclusion
Frame as: 'The local inhibition graph with fixed k=20 does not identify absorption pairs, suggesting inhibition operates at finer granularity'
Make clear this is an informative negative result, not a failure

action_items.description: n=4 layers insufficient for inferential statistics. r=+0.82 is descriptive. 'Aligns with prior findings' treats descriptive trend as supporting evidence.
specific_changes: Remove 'aligns with prior findings' language
Report as purely descriptive: 'A descriptive trend was observed (mean edge weight: L0=0.312, L8=0.384, L10=0.367). With n=4, no inferential statistics are appropriate.'

action_items.issue: Missing data source for H8 claim
action_items.description: Section 4.5 claims r=+0.12, p=0.55 for graph statistics predicting at-risk features. No data file confirmed to contain this.
specific_changes: Verify from h6_inhibition_graph.json or remove the claim
If verifiable, add citation: 'Source: exp/results/full/h6_inhibition_graph.json, per-feature graph statistics correlated with absorption rates'

action_items.description: Text references fig7_precision_recall.pdf but calls it 'Figure 3'. Table/figure numbering may be inconsistent.
specific_changes: Check all figure files exist in writing/latex/figures/ or exp/results/figures/
Ensure consistent figure numbering: Figure 1=LCA correspondence, Figure 2=suppression mechanism, Figure 3=precision-recall
Remove fig7_precision_recall.pdf reference or clarify it is Figure 3

action_items.description: The paper needs a formal Table 1 showing all 12 tests with Bonferroni and BH-FDR corrected p-values.
specific_changes: Generate table from correlation_report_full.json multiple_comparisons_correction section
Table should show: hypothesis, layer, metric, raw p, Bonferroni p, BH q, significant (Y/N)

action_items.description: The random vs trained absorption comparison (H10: 0.034 vs 0.278) should be a figure showing trained < random.
specific_changes: Generate from ablation_random_baseline.json
Figure should show: trained SAE mean absorption (0.034), random SAE mean absorption (0.278), error bars, statistical significance marker

action_items.description: All experiments use GPT-2 Small. Gemma-2-2B pilot incomplete. Generalizability claims cannot be made.
specific_changes: Either complete Gemma-2-2B experiment or add explicit limitation: 'All findings are specific to GPT-2 Small; cross-model validation awaits future work'
